text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in our daily lives, from hiring tools to loan applications. However, using AI raises important ethical concerns. One major issue is bias, which happens when an AI system treats certain groups unfairly because of flawed data. This directly relates to fairness—we must ensure AI decisions do not discriminate against anyone. Privacy is another key concern, as AI often collects large amounts of personal information without clear consent. Transparency is also essential; people need to understand how AI makes decisions. To address these challenges, responsible AI development involves creating systems that are ethical by design, with regular testing and oversight to protect users and society.
